title: DESS (DMSO/EDTA/NACL) Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"58ksi","text":"DESS (DMSO/EDTA/NACL) protocol instructing how to make the solution for the preservation of samples. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Combine 372.24 g disodium EDTA and 500 mL deionized water. Tip: Disodium EDTA is labeled PINK in the Bik Lab.

Add enough 5M NaOH to the solution to bring it to a pH of pH 8.0 Tip: NaOH is labeled GREEN in the Bik Lab. Tip: In some cases, this can be as much as 500 mL. The EDTA will begin to dissolve around a pH of pH 7.0 .
Bring final volume to 2 L with deionized water once all disodium EDTA has dissolved.

Combine 1 L 0.5M disodium EDTA , 400 mL Dimethyl Sulfoxide/DMSO , and 600 mL deionized water in a 4000 mL Erlenmeyer flask. Tip: 0.5M disodium EDTA is labeled YELLOW and Dimethyl Sulfoxide/DMSO is labeled ORANGE in the Bik Lab.

Add enough NaCl to saturate the solution: Add NaCl in 100g increments until 300g is reached, and then proceed to adding smaller amounts until salt is no longer dissolving. Solution should saturate with the addition of ~300-400g NaCl. Tip: NaCl is labeled RED in the Bik Lab. Tip: This step can take hours: BE PATIENT. There may be leftover salt residue once the solution is fully saturated.
Put magnetic stir bars in the solution and place flask onto a hot plate. Turn the magnetic stirring function on and leave it at room temperature.
creator: Jenna Brown
